title: Solipsism
description: The philosophical idea that only one's own mind is sure to exist.
definition: The epistemological position that the self is the only reality that can be verified and known. Knowledge of anything outside one's own mind is uncertain; the external world and other minds cannot be known and may not exist outside of one's own perceptions. It represents an extreme form of subjective idealism.

proofs.description: Roots in Descartes' 'Cogito ergo sum' and developed by Berkeley and later idealists.
The problem of other minds: we cannot directly experience the consciousness of others.
